preamble: The shift from 'sh' (ʃ) to 'ch' (tʃ) is an affrication process where a post-alveolar fricative gains a stop component, becoming an affricate.

examples.note: English loanwords in various languages often undergo affrication.
Common substitution in early language acquisition.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
